name: All-in-One Sausage and Zucchini Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 pound hot or mild Italian sausage
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. Heat olive oil in a large 5-quart Dutch oven over medium heat until hot but not smoking.
3. Add the ground chuck and sausage; cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 6-8 minutes. Use tongs or a spatula to transfer meat mixture to a plate or bowl set aside.
4. In the same pot, sauté zucchini, garlic, and onion over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until they start to soften but remain slightly crisp, with no color change.
5. Stir in rice; cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until it begins to absorb moisture and becomes translucent.
6. Add tomatoes, fennel seed, red pepper flakes, basil, rosemary, salt, and black pepper; stir well.
7. Pour in chicken broth or stock, bringing the mixture to a simmer without boiling over.
8. Stir in the reserved meat mixture until evenly distributed.
9. Spoon the casserole into a rectangular baking dish, spreading it out evenly.
10. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top of the dish.
11. Bake uncovered for 1 hour, or until the casserole is bubbly and the surface has developed a golden crust.
12. Optionally, sprinkle additional Parmesan cheese over the top before serving to garnish with extra flavor and texture.
13. This recipe can be made up to two days in advance; it also freezes well for later consumption. Thaw overnight in refrigerator if needed, then reheat covered until steaming just before serving.
